parts.description: An optional arrow element rendered alongside the preview-card.
An optional backdrop rendered behind the popup.

A container for the preview-card contents.

**ADR 0015 migration** onto the new floating dismissal engine (dismissal-only — a preview-card has
no focus manager, ADR 0017 §1). Escape, an outside press, and a focus-out all close it (the legacy's
trigger-press preventDefault is now automatic: the trigger is the registered reference, so a press on
it is "inside" and never fires `pointerDownOutside`). A focus-out while a pointer is held on the
trigger is still vetoed.

outputs.name: escapeKeyDown
outputs.description: Event handler called when the escape key is down. Can be prevented.

outputs.name: focusOutside
outputs.description: Event handler called when focus moves outside of the popup. Can be prevented.

outputs.name: interactOutside
outputs.description: Event handler called when an interaction happens outside of the popup. Can be prevented.

outputs.name: pointerDownOutside
outputs.description: Event handler called when a pointerdown event happens outside of the popup. Can be prevented.

parts.description:
Structural directive that teleports the preview-card content into a container (default
`document.body`) while the card is open, and keeps it mounted until any CSS exit `@keyframes`
finishes.

Apply it with the `*` microsyntax on the positioner —
`<div *rdxPreviewCardPortal rdxPreviewCardPositioner>` — or as an explicit
`<ng-template rdxPreviewCardPortal>`. For a custom container use the explicit form with `[container]`.

Dev-mode guard: `rdxPreviewCardPortal` used to be an attribute directive on a `<div>`. It is now
structural, so the old `<div rdxPreviewCardPortal>` markup would silently stop portaling — fail
loudly instead.

Positions the preview-card against its trigger.

A "thin" positioner (ADR 0012): it inherits the popper positioning surface (inputs, `placed`
output, unified vars + placement attrs) from {@link RdxPopperContentWrapper} and adds preview-card's
own concerns — Base UI-aligned defaults via the config provider, the open/closed/instant state
attributes, the deprecated `--radix-preview-card-*` aliases, and the grace-area hover bridge.

inputs.name: align
inputs.description: How to align the popup relative to the specified side. May change when collisions occur.

inputs.name: alignOffset
inputs.description:
An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options. Also accepts an
{@link OffsetFunction} (same signature as `sideOffset`).

inputs.name: anchor
inputs.description: An element to position the popup against. Defaults to the popper anchor.

inputs.name: arrowPadding
inputs.description:
Minimum distance to maintain between the arrow and the edges of the popup.
If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it from overflowing the corners.

inputs.name: avoidCollisions
inputs.description:
When `true`, overrides the `side` and `align` preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

`false` disables all avoidance (unless a `collisionAvoidance` object is provided, which wins).

inputs.name: collisionAvoidance
inputs.description:
How the popup avoids colliding with the boundary edges, per axis (Base UI `collisionAvoidance`).
Overrides the deprecated `avoidCollisions` and any per-primitive preset. An object here **fully
replaces** the preset — omitted fields fall back to `side: 'flip'`, `align: 'flip'`,
`fallbackAxisSide: 'end'`, never to the preset. See {@link RdxCollisionAvoidance}.

inputs.name: collisionBoundary
inputs.description:
The element used as the collision boundary.
By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional element(s) to be included in this check.

inputs.name: collisionPadding
inputs.description:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur.
Accepts a number (same for all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: `{ top: 20, left: 20 }`.

inputs.name: dir
inputs.description:
Text direction, used only to resolve a logical `side` (`inline-start` / `inline-end`) to a
physical one. Falls back to an enclosing direction provider, then `ltr`.

inputs.name: hideWhenDetached
inputs.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

inputs.name: positionStrategy
inputs.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

inputs.name: side
inputs.description:
The preferred side of the anchor to render against when open.
Will be reversed when collisions occur and avoidCollisions is enabled.

Accepts the logical `'inline-start'` / `'inline-end'` in addition to the physical sides; those
resolve to `left` / `right` for the text direction (Base UI parity). The placed side reported via
`data-side` / `placedSide()` echoes the requested kind: logical in → logical out (even after a
collision flip), physical in → physical out.

inputs.name: sideOffset
inputs.description:
Distance between the anchor and the popup in pixels. Also accepts an {@link OffsetFunction} that
reads the anchor / positioner dimensions and the resolved side / align.

inputs.name: sticky
inputs.description:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the
content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at least partially
in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary
regardless.

inputs.name: updatePositionStrategy
inputs.description: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

outputs.name: placed
outputs.description: Emits when the element is placed.

parts.description: Groups all parts of the preview-card.

inputs.name: defaultOpen
inputs.description: Whether the preview-card is initially open.

inputs.name: defaultTriggerId
inputs.description: ID of the trigger associated with an initially open uncontrolled preview-card.

inputs.name: handle
inputs.description: Associates this root with detached trigger elements.

inputs.name: open
inputs.description: Whether the preview-card is currently open.

inputs.name: triggerId
inputs.description: ID of the trigger associated with a controlled preview-card.

parts.description: A link or element that opens the preview card.

inputs.name: closeDelay
inputs.description: How long to wait before closing a hover-opened preview-card, in milliseconds.

inputs.name: delay
inputs.description: How long to wait before opening the preview-card on hover, in milliseconds.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: Whether the trigger should ignore user interaction.

inputs.name: handle
inputs.description: Associates this trigger with a detached preview-card root.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: ID used to identify this trigger when opening a detached preview-card imperatively.

inputs.name: payload
inputs.description: Data associated with this trigger while it is active.

parts.description:
A viewport for animating content changes when a preview-card moves between triggers.

Render one direct child inside the viewport. It is marked with `data-current`;
when the active trigger changes, a DOM snapshot is retained as `data-previous`
until its CSS animation or transition completes.
